rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture and insert annex content only within a valid owner scope.
Keep component-type contract content separate from implementation-level content; do not move declarations across scopes during fusion.
generic_rules: Attach added content to the intended owning component or implementation without altering unrelated features, subcomponents, connections, or properties.
Preserve original model text and structure as much as possible; perform the smallest structural merge needed.
Do not introduce references that are only valid in a different scope than the chosen owner.
If multiple AADL elements exist nearby, ensure the merged annex remains nested under the correct owner block.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Component type scopes normally hold contracts and declarations, while implementation scopes hold implementation-level bindings when valid there.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only references into a component-type scope.
Do not modify architecture elements such as features, subcomponents, connections, or property associations while inserting annex text.
irrelevant_topics: Validator type-fix strategies for bool/int/real declarations
AADL property names or unit tokens as AGREE expression variables
Requirement-level threshold semantics beyond ownership and scope preservation
